LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 301, AS TO THE JAN-21-2024 HEARING RE: DEFENDANT AMANDA INNOCENCIO SUED HEREIN AS AMANDA LYDIA INNOCENCIO'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS AS TO PLAINTIFF JASMINE GROTEGEER'S COMPLAINT; THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. GRANTED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. IF THE PLAINTIFF CHOOSES TO FILE AN AMENDED COMPLAINT, SHE MUST DO SO BY FEBRUARY 13, 2025. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING WITH A COVER LETTER OUTLINING COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. calendar.matters:
MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT .
Off Calendar May-15-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, JONATHON H. NEWSOME, AND SHARON ANDERSON'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendants the City and County of San Francisco, Jonathan H. Newsome, and Sharon Anderson move for summary judgment. Their moving papers carry their burden as the moving party on summary judgment of presenting evidence to show that there is no triable issue of material fact and they are entitled to judgment as a matter of law. Plaintiff Jasmine Grotegeer has not opposed the motion and has not shown a triable issue of fact. The court therefore GRANTS the motion. Defendants shall submit a proposed judgment of dismissal. Proposed order submitted for review. Judge: Christine Van Aken, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Have Requests For Admissions Deemed Admitted
Off Calendar Feb-04-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT JONATHAN NEWSOME's Motion To Have Requests For Admissions Deemed Admitted.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant Jonathan Newsome's motion for a deemed admitted order on the Requests for Admission she served on plaintiff Jasmine Grotegeer, attached as Exhibit A to the LaFleur Declaration, is granted. Grotegeer has not responded, making a deemed-admitted order mandatory. (Code Civ. Proc., sec. 2033.280, subd. (c).) Sanctions are also mandatory. (Id.) The court orders Grotegeer to pay a nominal sanction of $15 to Newsome within 30 days.

A proposed order has been submitted as indicated in the tentative ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (301/CVA)

Off Calendar Mar-20-2025 Continued To Jun-18-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorneys Fees
Off Calendar Apr-03-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
Megan Elizabeth McDonald, Esq., Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith 619-699-4901 megan.mcdonald@lewisbrisbois.com Attorney for Defendant ELISA MOLLICK
No other appearances.

Discussed, not ARGUED. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ELISA MOLLICK'S Motion For Attorneys Fees.

Moving party Elisa Mollick succeeded on a special motion to strike Plaintiff Jasmine Grotegeer's claims against her pursuant to CCP Sec. 425.16. She now seeks fees in the amount of $15,360. For the reasons stated herein the court grants this unopposed motion.

An award of fees and costs is mandatory to a prevailing defendant on an anti-SLAPP motion. (CCP 425.16 (c)(1).) "[A] defendant who brings a successful special motion to strike is entitled only to reasonable attorney fees, and not necessarily to the entire amount requested." (Cabral v. Martins (2009) 177 Cal.App.4th 471, 491.) To determine a reasonable amount, "[t]he amount of an attorney fee award under the anti-SLAPP statute is computed by the trial court in accordance with the familiar lodestar method. Under that method, the court tabulates the attorney fee touchstone, or lodestar, by multiplying the number of hours reasonably expended by the reasonable hourly rate prevailing in the community for similar work." (Id. [quotation marks and citations omitted].)

After review of the anti-SLAPP moving papers and the breakdown of fees set out in Exhibit E of the Graft Declaration, the court finds that the number of hours expended was reasonable. The court also finds that the hourly rates are reasonable based on paragraphs 12-17 of the Graft Declaration. Mollick is entitled to "fees on fees" - that is, fees she incurred in bringing this fee motion. (See Ketchum v. Moses (2001) 24 Cal.4th 1122, 1141.) Because the court finds the fee request reasonable using the lodestar method, it awards $15,360 to Mollick payable by Grotegeer.

Preavailing party to submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)
